notes: v1.14.119 - B3 HOTFIX (UI walkthrough, BUGS list): Command page wouldn't scroll far enough - the OPS SIGNAL section at the bottom was clipped behind the fixed bottom chrome and couldn't be brought fully into view. ROOT CAUSE (browser-verified at 412x915): #pg-cmd (the redesign Command page, the scroll container) had padding-bottom calc(96px + safe-bottom) = ~108px, but the bottom is obstructed by TWO fixed layers - the omni-bar (Log to active deployment; position:fixed; bottom:var(--tabnav-h); ~57px tall) sitting directly above #rd-botnav (~84px) = a 141px stack. 108 < 141, so the last ~33px of content (the tail of OPS SIGNAL) could never scroll above the omni-bar. FIX: padding-bottom -> calc(var(--tabnav-h) + 76px) (~160px) so the scroll runway clears the omni-bar + nav stack; OPS SIGNAL now scrolls fully into view above the omni-bar (verified: at max scroll the OPS SIGNAL header sits at y678, clear of the omni-bar top y774). CSS-only, one line. #pg-cmd is redesign-only (showMode 'cmd'); legacy (?legacy=1) never renders it -> byte-identical. Note: B2 (Command Lens 'ON SHIFT' pill floats absolute instead of in the card flow) is a placement error routed to the later UI-consistency sweep, not fixed here. node --check x3 + sw.js, brace gate, three-stamp lockstep, CRLF-preserving. DEVICE-VERIFY: Command page scrolls all the way down; OPS SIGNAL section fully visible above the Log bar; ?legacy=1 unchanged.
